data.title: EPF Scheme 2026 fixes ₹1,800 monthly PF limit; 17-year tenure stays crucial for investors
data.description:
NEW DELHI, July 2, 2026, 20:34 IST

  • India rolled out its new EPF Scheme on June 29, replacing the old 1952 plan.
  • PF stays at 12% each for workers and companies, with payments tied to the wage ceiling cap.
  • Investors aren’t as focused on the one-time enrolment plan for workers who’ve been excluded since April 2009.
  • When PF isn’t deducted from workers, most of the financial risk lands on employers rather than on employees.

India’s new provident fund rules have put a spotlight on two numbers for investors in labour-heavy companies: ₹1,800, which is now the standard monthly PF payment per side under the wage ceiling, and April 1, 2009, the new cut-off for a fresh enrolment period. The window lets firms fix any past gaps in coverage for older staff. The government notified the Employees’ Provident Fund Scheme, 2026 on June 29 under the Code on Social Security, 2020.

NSE equity trading did not open at the deadline. The exchange lists regular trading from 09:15 to 15:30 IST.

The Gazette reported the Employees’ Enrolment Campaign, 2026, is open until Oct. 31, 2026. Companies are allowed to enroll staff hired from April 1, 2009, through March 31, 2026, as long as they’re still on payroll when declared and haven’t been previously registered.

This matters for sectors like staffing, construction, retail and facility management where headcounts run high, because payrolls go through both contractors and main firms. Under this setup, the employer puts up both the employer and employee shares at once for workers on their own rolls or, in some cases, hired through a contractor.

The personal-finance angle is simple. The contribution rate stays at 12% of wages from both sides, but some notified establishments still use the 10% rate. Business Standard said the employee share is locked to the ₹15,000 statutory cap, so it comes out to ₹1,800 a month—any extra is voluntary.

The table skips the auto savings number. Instead, it gives the gap between the legal minimum and a full PF wage rule. India Today reports that employers match voluntary contributions above ₹1,800 only if the contract or company policy requires it.

Business Standard reported that workers paying the full PF on their actual salary likely won’t notice any difference in their pay-slips. There’s no automatic cut for those already contributing on real pay. Any sums above the cap can still go in as voluntary PF, if workers or employers choose, under the rules that stand now.

Puneet Gupta, Partner at EY India’s People Advisory Services, called the scheme an “important step toward implementing the labour codes” in comments to ANI. Gupta said companies face “enhanced governance and compliance obligations” now, including contractor compliance, ownership disclosure, more electronic filings, and dealing with exempted PF trusts. The Economic Times

Withdrawal rules mean more paperwork for admins. NewsOnAir said the scheme will change partial withdrawal terms covering medical expenses, education fees, marriage, and housing, setting a minimum balance and new requirements. Business Standard reported the list of withdrawal reasons will go from 13 down to three key categories.

PF, pension and insurance claims now have to be processed in 20 days under new rules, Upstox reported. If there’s an unjustified delay, the rules allow for a 12% annual penal interest. The penalty can be deducted from the Commissioner’s salary.

data.title: GE Vernova T&D India’s ₹1.27 lakh crore market cap raises questions on order-book premium
data.description:
BENGALURU, June 30, 2026, 02:32 IST

  • GE Vernova T&D India (NSE:GVT&D) ended Monday at ₹4,956.50, dropping 1.72%. Nifty 50 slipped 0.46% the same day.
  • The stock is trading at roughly 5.9x its March order backlog and 20.5x FY26 revenue, going by company filings and Monday’s market cap.
  • The ₹550 million capacity plan looks small compared to the market cap, but it shows the real test now is turning the backlog into actual supply and holding on to margins.

Indian stocks were closed at the cutoff. The National Stock Exchange’s regular cash session goes from 9:15 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. IST, so the last price for GE Vernova T&D India Limited (NSE:GVT&D) is Monday’s close.

The stock closed Monday at ₹4,956.50, off ₹86.50 or 1.72%. Shares traded between ₹5,079 and ₹4,721.50, a 7.1% move from the last close at ₹5,043. Weak finish for the stock, but it climbed about 5% off the session low.

It’s not the order book that’s holding the stock back. It’s what was paid to get it. GE Vernova T&D posted Q4 order bookings at ₹86.1 billion and put its order backlog at ₹214.6 billion as of March 2026. For FY26, revenue came in at ₹62.06 billion, with net profit at ₹12.33 billion.

This matters as a strong backlog-to-sales ratio helps support revenue, but it also puts pressure on execution. Orders need to move to billings fast enough to keep up the valuation. Cash conversion is also key. Operating cash flow for FY26 was ₹17.10 billion, compared to net profit of ₹12.33 billion.

Chief Executive Sandeep Zanzaria told analysts on the May earnings call that the order book stayed strong in Q4. The company reported ₹86.1 billion in Q4 bookings, a jump of 188% from last year.

The board cleared ₹550 million to boost capacity for disconnectors and drives for 362 kV dead tank circuit breakers at Vallam, Tamil Nadu. First production is expected by December 2026. Funding comes from internal accruals, according to the company.

The peer group moved in both directions on Monday, so the drop in GE Vernova T&D wasn’t just about the sector. Hitachi Energy India (NSE:POWERINDIA) climbed 2.97%, ABB India (NSE:ABB) was up 1.93%, while Siemens Energy India (NSE:ENRIN) slipped 2.76%.

The long run-up in the chart still means the stock has little margin for error. NSE figures show GE Vernova T&D has climbed 59.58% in 2026 to date and 115.21% in the past year, while Nifty 50 gained 11.63% and 9.56%. Over three years, GE Vernova T&D is up 2,266.45%, versus the index’s 63.61%.

Jefferies began covering GE Vernova T&D last week with a Hold and a ₹6,000 price target. The firm kept Buy on Hitachi Energy India and Siemens Energy India, saying those two offer clearer earnings growth in India’s power transmission story.

Indian shares dropped Monday, snapping a three-week rally. The Nifty 50 lost 0.46% and the Sensex gave up 0.48%. Saurabh Jain at SMC Global told Reuters “fresh upside is limited” as traders watch monsoon progress after weak June rain. Reuters

data.title: Relaxo Footwears (NSE:RELAXO) jumps 12% ahead of India market break, delivery data in focus
data.description:
NEW DELHI, June 28, 2026, 03:36 (IST)

  • Relaxo ended at ₹413.55 on June 25, rising 12.09% before Indian exchanges closed for Muharram and the weekend.
  • NSE saw volume at 33.75 million shares with traded value of ₹1,393.54 crore, while delivery came in at just 3.24%.
  • The stock rose 16.21% this week but is still 21% under its 52-week high of ₹526.
  • The focus this week is on follow-through, after Q4 growth was led by volume. Average realization per pair was down to ₹150.

Relaxo goes into Monday trading after Thursday’s 12% jump, but the question is how much of that move was real positioning versus short-term churn ahead of the three-day break.

The stock settled at ₹413.55 on June 25, adding ₹44.60 for the day, swinging from ₹373.10 to ₹423. At 11:46 IST, it led gains in BSE’s A group. Volume on BSE hit 11.92 lakh shares, far above the one-month average of 92,873 shares, according to Capital Market data from Axis Direct.

Delivery stayed low. Choice data showed NSE volume at 33.75 million shares and ₹1,393.54 crore in value, but just 3.24% was delivery. Traded value made up about 13.5% of Relaxo’s ₹10,294.86-crore market cap.

The stock missed its usual Friday session. BSE and NSE were closed June 26 for Muharram, with the weekend following. Indian equities only trade on weekdays, skipping Saturdays, Sundays, and official holidays.

Large-block trades are fueling churn instead of switching hands between big players. Microcurves Trading Private Limited traded 1.2978 million Relaxo shares both ways on June 25, data showed. The trades went through at about ₹413 per share, worth about ₹53.6 crore per side. That amount was under 4% of the NSE volume for the day, so it doesn’t account for all the action.

Relaxo shares jumped 16.21% this week, clawing back some ground on the longer chart. The stock ended at ₹413.55, still about 21% off its 52-week peak of ₹526. According to Economic Times data, it traded at 57.43 times earnings and 4.67 times book value.

Relaxo’s numbers offer bulls a bit of a cushion, though it’s slim. March-quarter revenue was up 8.1% at ₹751 crore, with EBITDA up 10.6% to ₹124 crore and profit after tax rising 20.4% to ₹68 crore. But for the year, revenue slipped 3.1% to ₹2,702 crore even as profit ticked up 5.3% to ₹179 crore.

Volume drove the recovery, according to the company’s data. Pairs sold jumped to 5.0 crore in the March quarter, up from 4.0 crore in December, hitting the top quarterly number in FY26. Average realization per pair dropped to ₹150 compared with ₹164, but EBITDA margin increased to 16.5% from 10.4%.

Chairman and managing director Ramesh Kumar Dua said in the May 28 release, “volume growth remained strong.” He said general trade recovered, while retail, e-commerce, and large-format retail picked up as well.

Dua said Relaxo “must tread with caution” as inflation could weigh on demand. The company has raised prices to deal with higher input costs, but the release said the “full impact on demand and consumption patterns is still evolving.”

June 25’s range comes up as the first test on Monday. Clearing ₹423 keeps the rally going after the holiday. But if it slips back toward ₹373.10, that likely signals profit-taking after a spike in low-delivery trades.

Relaxo’s board flagged a ₹3.50 a share dividend and put the record date at Sept. 18. But the speed bump now is market action. Turnover jumped well past delivery last week—watch if buyers come back.

data.title: Infosys stock near 52-week low; ADR rebound puts AI pullback to the test
data.description:
BENGALURU, June 28, 2026, 01:36 (IST)

  • Infosys Limited (NSE:INFY) closed at ₹1,041.20 on Thursday in India, dropping 1.46%, ahead of the NSE holiday for Muharram on June 26.
  • The stock closed the holiday-shortened week only 1.5% higher than its 52-week low of ₹1,026.
  • Infosys’ U.S. ADR (NYSE:INFY) added 1.99% to $10.78 on Friday. That sets up Monday’s local trade as a straightforward catch-up move.
  • Indian IT stocks are still lagging. The group is off around 29% this year, Reuters said, while the Nifty 50 lost 8.3%.

Infosys Limited (NSE:INFY) heads into this week barely above its 52-week low after trading paused for the Muharram holiday on Friday. The National Stock Exchange listed June 26 as a holiday, so Infosys shares in India missed the Friday U.S. move that boosted its ADR.

Shares finished Thursday at ₹1,041.20, falling 1.46% as 15.87 million shares changed hands. The close put it ₹15.20 over the 52-week low of ₹1,026 but around 40% off the 52-week high of ₹1,728, per Economic Times market data.

Infosys traded unevenly through the four-day week. Shares added 1.33% Monday, dropped 3.39% Tuesday, recovered 2.65% Wednesday, then slipped 1.46% on Thursday. The stock moved between ₹1,080.30 and ₹1,026, ending Thursday near the bottom of that range.

ADR closed up 1.99% at $10.78 Friday, while the S&P 500 lost 0.05% and the Dow dropped 0.09%. The question for locals Monday is whether Friday’s buying in the U.S. was just a quick rebound, or if support is building around the Indian low.

Nifty 50 edges up, Sensex gains as traders cut positions ahead of Friday closure

The Nifty 50 finished up 0.14% at 24,056 on Thursday, while the BSE Sensex also rose 0.14% to 77,100.47. Over the week, the Nifty 50 moved up 0.2% and the Sensex climbed 0.4%. “As markets will be shut on Friday, investors trimmed their positions in late trades,” said Ankur Punj, managing director and business head at Equirus Wealth. Reuters

IT stocks took a hit. India’s Nifty IT index dropped to a three-year low on June 19 after Accenture (NYSE:ACN) delivered a weak forecast, missing Wall Street’s sales estimates, cutting its full-year outlook and showing soft managed-services bookings. Tata Consultancy Services (NSE:TCS), Infosys and HCL Technologies (NSE:HCLTECH) fell between 4% and 8% that session.

Pritesh Thakkar, equity analyst at PL Capital, said to Reuters that while Indian IT companies don’t have much direct exposure to the Middle East, they still run into indirect risks. Those include delays in closing deals and slower pace in projects or client decisions. Mayuresh Joshi, head of equity research at William O’Neil & Co, said the market is looking for growth, but that is “clearly missing.” Reuters

Infosys is sticking to its line that AI brings more business to IT service firms, not less. Speaking at the company’s annual meeting on June 23, Chairman Nandan Nilekani said, “AI will not replace a company like ours.” He added, “The AI deployment gap in large enterprise clients is real, and closing that gap is where the work is.” Moneycontrol

Infosys reported $20.16 billion in revenue for fiscal 2026, a 3.1% rise in constant currency. Large-deal total contract value landed at $14.9 billion. For fiscal 2027, the company is guiding for constant currency revenue growth between 1.5% and 3.5% and sees operating margin in a 20% to 22% range.

Infosys (₹1,026) starts the week with that level as first support—falling under it means a new 52-week low. The upside marker is ₹1,056.60, enough to erase Thursday’s drop, while a finish above ₹1,080.30 sets a short-week high. The company has no earnings report until July 23, so market direction will mostly track its ADR, sector peers and the Nifty IT index moves in the days ahead.

data.title: Reliance Industries stock: Jio IPO math holds back rally, market looks at holding discount
data.description:
MUMBAI, June 27, 2026, 23:38 IST

  • Reliance Industries Limited (NSE:RELIANCE) closed the holiday week at ₹1,318.10, gaining 0.7% from June 19.
  • NSE and BSE didn’t open Friday for Muharram and stay closed all weekend.
  • Jio’s filing shows a minor fresh issue but puts a big valuation mark on RIL’s embedded stake.
  • Jio’s price-band hints, Sebi’s DRHP review, and crude are set to drive the week ahead.

Reliance Industries Limited (NSE:RELIANCE) gets its next test on Monday when Indian trading resumes. The focus is on Jio Platforms’ listing numbers. Investors will see if that bumps up Reliance’s value, or if RIL gets hit with a bigger holding-company discount.

Muharram kept India’s equity, equity derivatives and securities lending markets closed on Friday at both NSE and BSE. Trading is set to pick up again Monday, June 29, after the long break, Moneycontrol said.

RIL finished June 25 at ₹1,318.10, up 0.34%. That’s just 0.66% above its June 19 close at ₹1,309.50. Shares climbed to ₹1,344.90 on June 22 when the Jio filing hit the market but then slipped, losing about 2% from that peak.

Jio’s planned IPO is about valuation, not an exit for RIL. The DRHP says the IPO will be a fresh issue of up to 270 million shares, with money going to repay or prepay Reliance Jio Infocomm’s borrowings and for general corporate use. RIL owns 5.94 billion Jio shares, or 66.43%, before the IPO. If the full issue is sold, that will fall to around 64.5%.

Jio’s IPO is targeting around ₹360 billion ($3.81 billion), according to Reuters, which is about 2.9% of post-issue equity and puts Jio’s valuation close to $131 billion. Mukesh Ambani called the Jio IPO “the most important value creation milestone this year” during the AGM. Reuters

RIL closed June 25 with a market cap near ₹17.84 lakh crore. Reuters’ IPO breakdown puts Jio’s implied post-money valuation near ₹12.4 lakh crore, leaving RIL’s post-issue stake valued at about ₹8.0 lakh crore—roughly 45% of RIL’s total market value.

Piyush Pandey at Centrum told ET Now a 2.9% fresh equity issue is small and “should not be difficult to put it through.” After the listing, he expects the holding-company discount could run “around 20 to 25%.” The Economic Times

RIL found some shelter as the Nifty 50 (INDEXNSE:NIFTY_50) picked up 0.2% this week and the BSE Sensex (INDEXBOM:SENSEX) edged up 0.4%, according to Reuters, with Brent crude settling at $72.70 a barrel. “As markets will be shut on Friday, investors trimmed their positions in late trades,” said Ankur Punj, managing director and business head at Equirus Wealth. Reuters

Sebi has asked Jio Platforms for more details on its DRHP, The Economic Times said June 25. The company still needs Sebi’s final observations before it can move forward with the offer.

RIL faces a key week as investors look to where the stock goes next, with Jio’s price band still not set and details on the issue still missing. The company hasn’t said when Jio plans to launch the issue.

data.title: HDFC Bank (NSE:HDFCBANK) still ₹3.45 lakh crore below its 52-week high, July data ahead
data.description:
MUMBAI, June 26, 2026, 15:34 IST

  • Indian markets were closed Friday for Muharram. HDFC Bank finished Thursday at ₹796.30, gaining 0.40%.
  • The stock put up a 72.61-point gain for Nifty Bank on June 25. That’s nearly 2.7 times what the index itself ended with, a final gain of 26.70 points.
  • The average broker target on Trendlyne is ₹1,022.60, just above the 52-week high of ₹1,020.50 for the stock.
  • HDFC Bank will report its June-quarter earnings on July 18.

Muharram kept Indian stock markets shut on Friday, leaving investors with a three-day window to reassess HDFC Bank Limited (NSE:HDFCBANK) after shares bounced off lows. HDFC Bank last ended at ₹796.30 on June 25, a rise of 0.40% on the session and 2.12% for the week. Its market cap stood at ₹12,25,774 crore, according to Economic Times data.

The price is trading about 22% under its 52-week high of ₹1,020.50. According to figures from Economic Times, getting back to that level would add nearly ₹3.45 lakh crore to the bank’s market value, assuming share count doesn’t change. That’s the rerating on the table as investors look to the bank’s next results.

HDFC Bank was a big name for the bank index Thursday, even as the market stayed flat. It put 72.61 points into the Nifty Bank’s gains, second to ICICI Bank Limited (NSE:ICICIBANK). The index added just 26.70 points, or 0.05%, to finish at 58,177.05, after hitting 58,706.05. Axis Bank Limited (NSE:AXISBANK) and AU Small Finance Bank Limited (NSE:AUBANK) were drags.

HDFC Bank can still steady the bank index thanks to its size, but investors are watching the stock. The shares haven’t clawed back the valuation lost when governance concerns surfaced at the lender earlier this year.

Bank Nifty faces resistance at 58,500, Enrich Money CEO Ponmudi R told Moneycontrol. He said a strong move above that could push the index to 59,000-59,200. Amol Athawale, vice president for technical research at Kotak Securities, pegged support at 57,800 and 57,500. A close above those levels may help the rally extend to 58,700-59,000, he said.

HDFC Bank’s average target price is near its last high. Trendlyne puts the consensus at ₹1,022.60, about 28.42% above Tuesday’s close of ₹796.30, based on 33 reports from 12 analysts. That’s right next to the 52-week high, so the bullish view hinges mostly on the stock regaining its lost multiple.

HDFC Bank is set to hold a board meeting on July 18 to review unaudited standalone and consolidated results for the quarter ending June 30, according to a filing cited by Upstox. The trading window for designated staff and their close family will remain shut from June 24 until July 20, the bank said.

HDFC Bank’s last quarter sets the benchmark. Standalone net profit climbed 9.11% to ₹19,221.05 crore for Q4 FY26. Total income edged up 0.35% to ₹89,808.9 crore, Upstox reported, citing the company filing. Markets will focus next on deposit numbers, loan growth, and any commentary on margins for the June quarter.

HDFC Bank raised $750 million last week, taking bids for a five-year dollar bond that was priced at 90 basis points over U.S. Treasuries, offering a 5.067% yield, according to Reuters. It’s the biggest offshore bond sale by an Indian bank since State Bank of India (NSE:SBIN) placed a similar $750 million five-year issue in May 2023. Funding remains a focus for the market.

RBI is letting Keki Mistry stay on as interim chairman of HDFC Bank for another three months, Reuters said on June 18. Mistry stepped in after Atanu Chakraborty quit in March. Reuters reported Chakraborty’s exit wiped out around 14% from HDFC Bank’s stock in a few sessions and took $16 billion off its market cap.

HDFC Bank’s fall in 2026 is “not a fundamental story, it’s a governance discount,” Angel One senior fundamental analyst Vaqarjaved Khan told Economic Times earlier this month. He said the franchise remains strong and net interest margin recovery will be “a FY27 story.” But according to Khan, the market is right to price in a discount until trust gets better. The Economic Times

data.title: Chennai gold cuts two-day slide by just 20%, silver rebounds more
data.description:
CHENNAI, June 26, 2026, 13:33 IST

  • Chennai 22-carat gold gained ₹640 to hit ₹105,760 per sovereign, snapping a two-day drop of ₹3,360.
  • Silver jumped ₹5,000 to ₹235,000 per kg, clawing back about a quarter of losses from the last two sessions.
  • Morning trade at India’s MCX (NSE:MCX) was closed for Moharram, but the evening session for commodities was shown as open.

Chennai jewellery gold prices edged higher on Friday. The gain clawed back just a bit of ground lost earlier, as bullion had slid near its June lows.

Gold prices moved higher, with 22-carat gold up ₹80 per gram at ₹13,220 and the price for an 8-gram sovereign climbing ₹640 to ₹105,760, according to Daily Thanthi. Silver added ₹5 to ₹235 per gram. The per-kg price rose ₹5,000 to ₹235,000.

Gold rebounded Friday, recouping 19.0% of the ₹3,360 per sovereign loss from the last two sessions. Silver also traded higher, gaining back 25.0% of its recent ₹20,000 per kg slide, local rate data from Dinamani, Daily Thanthi and Dinamalar showed.

Chennai’s retail market is active but the national benchmark is not showing much. MCX named June 26 a Moharram holiday for the morning session, though markets still open at 5 p.m. IBJA called Friday a “Holiday” and did not post any AM or PM rates. MCXWEB

IBJA’s latest PM quote on June 25 had 916-purity gold at ₹128,124 per 10 grams, or ₹12,812.40 a gram. Retail prices in Chennai for 22-carat gold on Friday ran about 3.2% above that. IBJA put silver at ₹216,541 per kg, while the Chennai quote was ₹235,000. IBJA calls its prices benchmark rates for sovereign gold bonds and loans against jewelry from banks and NBFCs.

Local prices moved up, while overseas markets lagged. Spot gold dropped 0.5% to $4,007.95 an ounce by 0610 GMT Friday and was heading for a 3.6% loss on the week, according to Reuters. August U.S. gold futures slipped 0.6% to $4,024.10.

“The rapid repricing of the hawkish Fed created a strong bullish momentum in the U.S. dollar,” said Kelvin Wong, senior market analyst at OANDA, according to Reuters. Wong said the correction could go to $3,400 over the longer term. Reuters

Chennai gold prices haven’t recovered to early-month levels. Daily Thanthi reported a sovereign at ₹116,000 on June 1, but Friday’s price was ₹105,760, down ₹10,240, or 8.8%.

The drop trims ticket sizes for jewellers heading into the next full trading week and sets a lower collateral value for gold-loan lenders after a stretch of high rupee prices. The World Gold Council said India’s gold demand rose 10% year on year in Q1 2026 to 151 tonnes, but jewellery volumes fell 19%. Investment demand jumped 54% to 82 tonnes.

Silver’s bounce in local trade is moving faster, catching traders’ eyes as the international selloff has been more severe. Reuters reported spot silver dropped 9.1% on June 24 to $56.41, its lowest since November 2025.

Independent metals trader Tai Wong told Reuters that gold has “support just under $3,900” and said central bank buying probably rules out a collapse, but he’s looking for a drawn-out consolidation. Reuters

Gold and silver will likely stay choppy around the U.S. GDP and core PCE numbers, Manoj Kumar Jain of Prithvi Finmart told the Economic Times. He put MCX gold support between ₹140,000 and ₹138,800 for 10 grams, with resistance at ₹142,400 to ₹143,350. Jain said he is not buying the dip at these levels.

data.title: Micron shares up, Apple stuck with higher AI memory cost
data.description:
NEW YORK, June 25, 2026, 17:04 EDT

  • Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) lost roughly $270 billion in market cap on Thursday. That drop was larger than the combined gains from Micron (NASDAQ:MU) and Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM).
  • Micron said its revenue outlook for the current quarter points to a sequential rise to about $8.5 billion, which is close to what it booked in the year-ago period.
  • Apple hiked some MacBook and iPad prices by $100 to $300, with higher memory costs hitting consumer hardware.

Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) shed about $270 billion in market value on Thursday, while Micron Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:MU) and Qualcomm Inc. (NASDAQ:QCOM) together picked up around $195 billion, according to latest quoted market caps after the U.S. close. Shares of Micron jumped 15.6%, Qualcomm rose 3.8%, and Apple dropped 6.3%.

Split lets investors see the AI hardware story more clearly. The memory shortage that helped Micron’s profit view is the same force that made Apple raise prices. AI infrastructure demand is now hitting consumer device makers with higher costs.

Micron’s revenue in the fiscal third quarter jumped to $41.46 billion, up from $23.86 billion in the previous quarter and $9.30 billion in the same period last year. The chipmaker set guidance for current-quarter revenue at $50.0 billion, plus or minus $1.0 billion, and put its non-GAAP EPS forecast at $31.00, also plus or minus $1.00. CEO Sanjay Mehrotra said the results show the “strategic value of memory in the AI era.” GlobeNewswire

The other number to watch is the jump forecasted for next quarter. If Micron hits the middle of its guidance, fourth-quarter revenue would be up around $8.5 billion over the third quarter. That’s close to what it reported—$9.30 billion—in last year’s fourth quarter.

Micron is moving to secure the memory cycle. Reuters said the company got $22 billion in commitments across 16 strategic customer deals. The agreements include take-or-pay conditions, cash deposits, and price floors. Chief Business Officer Sumit Sadana told Reuters that the five-year take-or-pay contracts were new for the industry.

Apple raised prices across several products. The MacBook Neo now starts at $699, up from $599. The 512GB MacBook Air is listed at $1,299, versus $1,099 before. Apple also bumped the 128GB iPad Air to $749 from $599, AP reported with the new price list.

Apple said it had “never seen a component price increase this much, this quickly.” Ben Bajarin, CEO at Creative Strategies, said the memory market is “structurally tough for the foreseeable future.” IDC’s Nabila Popal said, “The iPhone isn’t spared, its hike is coming.” Reuters

TrendForce had already warned that conventional DRAM contract prices are set to jump 58% to 63% quarter over quarter in the second quarter, while NAND Flash contract prices could climb 70% to 75%, as suppliers push more capacity to HBM and server demand.

Qualcomm shares kept climbing after the chipmaker lifted its fiscal 2029 non-handset revenue target to $40 billion and put its data-center revenue goal above $15 billion. That new non-handset target is about double the company’s previous outlook.

Qualcomm touted a workaround on costs, too. Reuters said Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) will tap Qualcomm’s High Bandwidth Compute chips. Those chips use lower-cost memory from phones and laptops, not the pricey Nvidia-linked high-bandwidth memory. “Performance per cost advantage,” said Tony Pialis, who leads Qualcomm’s data center business. Reuters

Semis were hot but the rest of tech slipped. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index jumped 3.2%. Still, the Nasdaq Composite lost 0.46% and the S&P 500 finished just about unchanged, as a selloff in Apple and big tech kept a lid on gains from chips. “For Micron to generate the kinds of earnings and revenues they do, it’s coming out of somebody else’s hide,” said Carol Schleif, chief investment officer at BMO Family Office. Reuters

Micron is guiding to capital spending near $10 billion this quarter, topping the street’s $8.89 billion consensus. Apple hasn’t detailed any action other than raising prices to offset the memory impact.

data.title: Bharti Airtel (NSE:BHARTIARTL) drops 3.1% after Jio leads in M2M-adjusted May numbers
data.description:
Mumbai, June 26, 2026, 01:15 IST

  • Airtel dropped 3.1% over the four sessions this week, ending at 1,850.70 rupees.
  • Without the rounded M2M changes, estimated May consumer SIM adds are 1.11 million for Airtel and 1.33 million for Jio.
  • S&P upgraded Airtel to BBB+. Markets in India are shut Friday for Muharram.

Bharti Airtel Limited dropped 1.42% to close at 1,850.70 rupees on Thursday, falling for a third day. Shares were down 3.1% for the week. The Nifty 50 edged up 0.2%, marking another weekly gain. Markets will be shut Friday for the Muharram holiday, with trading back Monday.

Airtel led subscriber growth in the May numbers from India’s telecom regulator, picking up 2,926,907 mobile users. Reliance Jio Infocomm, under Reliance Industries Limited (NSE:RELIANCE), came in with 2,154,468 new connections. Airtel’s mobile market share hit 37.89%, while Jio kept 39.27%.

The total includes machine-to-machine SIMs. Airtel’s M2M numbers reached 80.83 million in April, up from 79.01 million a month before, rising by 1.82 million. Jio’s M2M base was at 25.10 million, climbing from 24.28 million, a gain of 820,000.

With the rounded M2M numbers stripped out, Airtel’s consumer SIM adds came in at about 1.11 million. Jio added around 1.33 million. That put Jio ahead of Airtel by about 228,000 consumer SIMs, even though its total mobile base lagged on the regulator’s combined count.

India added around 1.46 million regular SIMs in May, but machine-to-machine (M2M) SIMs jumped by 3.63 million. That means about 71% of new net mobile connections came from machines, not people, according to the .

Airtel closed the month with 483.81 million mobile connections, out of which 80.83 million were M2M lines. Jio counted 501.44 million, including 25.10 million M2M. That puts Airtel at 402.98 million consumer SIMs and Jio at 476.34 million, giving them about 35.2% and 41.6% shares of the Indian consumer-SIM market, respectively. Airtel led on active-subscriber ratio, reporting 99.28% against Jio’s 98.83%.

S&P says the split is important for its forecast. The ratings agency thinks Airtel’s India subscribers will go up 3%-4% in the next year. It’s also looking for average revenue per user to gain 5%-7%. Consumer-SIM data lets investors see if those numbers hold up better than the broader mobile total.

S&P has lifted Airtel’s rating to BBB+ from BBB. The agency is looking for consolidated EBITDA to grow 8%-10% annually over the next two years and sees funds from operations to debt climbing above 50%. Airtel announced the move after the market closed Wednesday. Shares lost 1.42% on Thursday.

Airtel’s Africa share swap hit this week, adding dilution. The company gave out 146,761,335 new shares to Indian Continent Investment Limited, which is 2.35% of its capital after the issue. In return, Airtel got 595,204,251 shares in Airtel Africa Plc (LON:AAF), bumping up its stake in the London-listed arm to 79.22%.

Airtel Africa posted revenue of 160.3 billion rupees for the March quarter, up 40.9% from last year. Airtel India reported ARPU at 257 rupees. The number of its 4G and 5G customers increased 7.2%.

Trading picks up again June 29. Airtel goes into Monday after dropping three days in a row, with shares moving from 1,848 to 1,889 rupees on Thursday. “As markets will be shut on Friday, investors trimmed their positions in late trades,” said Ankur Punj, managing director and business head at Equirus Wealth. StockAnalysis

data.title: InterGlobe Aviation (NSE:INDIGO) jumps ₹18,900 crore after oil falls, easing IndiGo’s fuel risk
data.description:
Bengaluru, June 25, 2026, 17:36 (IST)

  • Shares ended the session at ₹5,450, rising 4.66%. The stock hit ₹5,464.90 during the day.
  • Shares added 9.85% over two sessions, lifting market value by about ₹18,900 crore.
  • Brent held close to $72.3 a barrel. The rupee ended at 94.3950 against the dollar.
  • Indian stock markets are closed Friday for Muharram, according to .

InterGlobe Aviation traded up on Thursday, finishing at ₹5,450 after starting the week at ₹4,961.40 on Tuesday. The stock climbed 4.66% Thursday after posting a 4.95% rise the session before.

IndiGo’s market cap rose by about ₹18,900 crore at Thursday’s close. The gain is around 74% of the airline’s ₹25,389 crore fuel bill for fiscal 2026. Fourth-quarter fuel costs slipped 1.5% year-on-year. But forex flipped to a ₹4,823 crore loss from a ₹137 crore gain.

For comparison, a 10% drop in the fiscal 2026 fuel bill—assuming no change in capacity or the rupee—would save close to ₹2,539 crore before tax. Shares jumped 7.4 times that number in just two days. This isn’t an earnings forecast. The stock now assumes fuel savings stick for more than one pricing period, or that another big cost gets cut.

IndiGo is watching the dollar. Over 60% of its costs tie back to the greenback. “We will be putting our minds to start looking at whether fuel hedging is another option,” Chief Financial Officer Gaurav Negi said after earnings. IndiGo sees first-quarter capacity growth at 3%-4%, much slower than the 16.4% seen a year ago. Reuters

Brent crude dropped to $72.66 on Thursday, the lowest since February 27, after roughly 20 million barrels moved through the Strait of Hormuz in a day. “Most of the increase in flows from the Gulf is outbound,” UBS (NYSE:UBS) analyst Giovanni Staunovo said. UBS has cut its Brent targets for end-September and end-December to $85 a barrel, which is still about 17% above where Thursday’s price settled. Reuters

Rupee up 0.3%, giving airlines some extra cost relief. Morningstar (NASDAQ:MORN) analyst Nicolas Owens said, “Sudden movements in fuel prices mean that in the near term the airline’s profitability can change in the opposite direction of the fuel price.” Airlines may not see the benefit right away, since tickets were sold based on earlier fuel costs. Reuters

IndiGo may not keep all of its savings if fare pressure rises. Civil Aviation Minister K. Ram Mohan Naidu said the government could tell airlines to look at surcharges and extra fares if crude prices stay lower. “Once we are clear that price stability will continue for a long time, we will talk to them,” he said. The Economic Times

InterGlobe jumped 8.5% this week, while the Nifty 50 added just 0.2%. With both the NSE and BSE closed Friday, markets will wait until Monday to react to three days of oil and Strait of Hormuz headlines.

data.title: Tanishq Gold Price Moves to Premium While MCX Selloff Hits Retail
data.description:
NEW DELHI, June 25, 2026, 13:57 (IST)

  • Tanishq’s 22K rate is down 3.1% since Tuesday. Rival quotes slipped 3.9%.
  • Tanishq’s gold moved from a 70-rupee-per-gram discount to a 45-rupee premium
  • MCX gold slipped 4.0% over two sessions, while silver dropped 6.9%

TITAN’s Tanishq dropped its 22-carat gold rate to 12,890 rupees a gram Thursday, down from 13,300 on Tuesday, after India’s bullion rout hit jewellery counters. That’s a 3.1% drop. Competitors Kalyan Jewellers (NSE:KALYANKJIL), Malabar Gold & Diamonds and Joyalukkas cut their price more, now at 12,845 from 13,370, off 3.9%. Tanishq moved from a 70-rupee discount to a 45-rupee premium in two days—a swing of 115 rupees.

Gold futures for August delivery on Multi Commodity Exchange of India Ltd (NSE:MCX) traded at 140,666 rupees per 10 grams by 12:28 p.m., dropping 5,863 rupees in two sessions, or 4.0%. July silver was down 15,526 rupees at 210,308 rupees per kg, a 6.9% decrease. Tanishq reflected about 77% of the gold futures’ percentage loss, while other listed rates tracked about 98%.

Futures gold-silver ratio climbed to 66.9 from 64.9 over those two sessions, up 3.1%. A check using purity conversion: MCX’s standard contract comes at 995 fineness, and Thursday’s 22K quotes work out to roughly 139,915 rupees per 10 grams at Tanishq, 139,427 rupees for peers. Both quotes landed inside 0.9% of the August future. Taxes, making charges, and futures carry aren’t part of this comparison; this isn’t an arbitrage.

ETFs tracked the same moves early today. Silver ETFs dropped almost 4%, gold ETFs fell around 2%. July silver futures slipped to near 210,000 rupees per kg, then bounced back over 215,000 rupees.

Silver kept dropping in Delhi’s physical market on Wednesday. Gold 99.9% purity lost 0.8% at 148,100 rupees per 10 grams. Silver dropped 1.7% to 231,000 rupees per kg, after falling by 10,500 rupees the previous day. “This is one of those rare periods where both equities and gold are declining together,” said Jateen Trivedi, LKP Securities analyst. Moneycontrol

Gold and silver fell again Thursday, with spot gold down 0.5% at $3,980.88 an ounce. Silver dropped 1% to $56.85 by 0738 GMT. Gold is now 29% under its record from January 29. “Gold is simply in a bearish momentum trade,” said Matt Simpson, senior analyst at StoneX. Reuters

Dollar index hovered around 101.5 after hitting 101.8, the strongest in 13 months. “The Fed is sending hawkish signals,” said OCBC’s Moh Siong Sim. The index crossed key resistance as traders moved from betting on U.S. rate cuts to pricing in a possible rate hike this year. Reuters

The rupee strengthened, adding to the local price drop. It gained up to 0.5% to 94.16 per dollar, its firmest level since early May. Brent crude slipped to $72.50 as policy moves drew in more dollars. “We expect the USD/INR to remain stable around 95.0 in the near term,” ANZ economist Dhiraj Nim said. Reuters

Gold jewellers put out Thursday’s figures with the rate shock still playing out. August gold stood at 144,676 rupees at 12:10 p.m. Wednesday. By 11:28 a.m. Thursday, it was at 141,238 rupees, dipping later to 140,666 rupees. Wednesday’s turnover hit 1,585 lots, while Thursday’s read 1,279 lots at the time of reporting.

Ravi Singh, chief research officer at Master Capital Services, said a drop below 145,000 rupees could trigger fresh selling, with support levels coming in at 140,000 and 136,000. Manoj Kumar Jain from Prithvifinmart told traders not to bottom-fish here, but said oversold conditions mean shorts should be covered.

data.title: HDFC Bank moves up in Mumbai with Indian banking pack, eyes on margin stress
data.description:
Mumbai, June 16, 2026, 20:36 IST

  • HDFC Bank ended the session 0.98% higher at ₹784.90, outpacing a firm Indian market.
  • Lower oil prices boosted risk appetite and helped the rally. Still, the stock remains down a lot in 2026.
  • Attention turns to the June-quarter update. Investors are watching deposit growth, loan growth and net interest margin.

HDFC Bank Limited closed 0.98% up at ₹784.90 on Tuesday as India’s main indexes gained. The stock had ended at ₹777.35 on Monday, according to the The Economic Times live stock page. Shares remain well below the 52-week high of ₹1,020.50. The 52-week low stands at ₹726.65. The Economic Times

Stocks in India pushed higher after the early U.S.-Iran peace deal cut oil prices and helped risk appetite. The Nifty 50 added 0.57% to finish at 23,989.15, and BSE Sensex climbed 0.71% to 76,808.48, Reuters said. HDFC Bank and Reliance Industries drove most of the gains in the benchmarks. Vinit Bolinjkar, head of research at Ventura Securities, said lower crude oil “augurs well for domestic markets,” easing the strain on Indian banks from inflation and the current account. Reuters

HDFC Bank is back in focus after The Economic Times reported law firms hired by the bank saw “no merit” in complaints filed by former chairman Atanu Chakraborty, citing sources. The report said their findings are expected to reach the audit committee. HDFC Bank didn’t respond. The news might clear up some governance clouds, but this isn’t a boost for earnings. The Economic Times

Growth quality is still the main issue for investors since the HDFC Ltd merger. In Q4 FY26, HDFC Bank reported average deposits of ₹28.511 trillion, up 12.8% year-on-year. Average advances stood at ₹29.644 trillion, a 10% rise. Profit after tax hit ₹192 billion. Net interest margin was 3.38%. Gross non-performing assets came in at 1.15%. NIM measures the difference between income from lending and funding costs, while gross NPA is the share of loans that are stressed before provisions. Reuters noted after the April numbers that HDFC Bank’s NIM hasn’t returned to the 4% level it had before the 2023 merger, so margin recovery is still on the radar for investors waiting for a stock rerating. Reuters

HDFC Bank bulls say the story’s straightforward. It’s India’s top private bank, asset quality looks steady, capital adequacy is at 19.7%. After a rough year, the stock isn’t expensive. The Economic Times shows a trailing P/E of 15.89. That P/E measures the price against earnings. There are risks too. Shares are still down for 2026, with margins softer since the merger. Some investors wonder if deposit growth can deliver without lifting funding costs. The Economic Times

HDFC Bank shares are still not cheap, though some long-term investors may spot value if they think deposits, margins and asset quality will pick up by FY27. Short-term trading remains dicey following a recent rally that lifted the stock in line with the broader market. Traders are now eyeing the June-quarter numbers and Q1 FY27 results. The main focus will be on deposit growth, loan growth, NIM and bad loans. The ₹13 dividend has a record date of June 19, which could push some trading ahead of that, but this doesn’t address core profitability. The Economic Times

data.title: MTAR Technologies Up 4.8% After Bloom Risk Drops, Valuation Remains Rich
data.description:
Mumbai, June 16, 2026, 16:20 (IST)

  • MTAR Technologies ended at ₹7,698.50 on the NSE, gaining 4.83%. The stock beat the Indian market today.
  • Shares rebounded after management said it hasn’t heard about any delays connected to Bloom Energy. That gave investors some relief.
  • The stock’s order book is solid, but the valuation leaves little cushion if the company misses on execution.

MTAR Technologies Ltd extended gains Tuesday, ending at ₹7,698.50 on the NSE, up ₹354.50 or 4.83%. On the BSE, the stock closed at ₹7,688.55, up 4.51%. Mirae Asset Sharekhan said the shares moved between ₹7,285.50 and ₹7,779 on the NSE, with volumes of about 3.19 million and a market cap nearing ₹23,680 crore. Indian equities moved up as well. The Nifty 50 added 0.57% at 23,989.15, while the Sensex gained 0.71% to end at 76,808.48. Reuters said sentiment improved on lower West Asia risk and softer oil prices. Sharekhan

Bloom Energy risks weighed on MTAR shares last week after a sharp drop, with focus on worries over possible delays at a Wyoming data center using Bloom’s fuel cells. MTAR relies on Bloom for big orders of fuel-cell parts. Moneycontrol reported Bloom accounts for 55–65% of MTAR’s annual revenue. Still, Managing Director Srinivas Reddy told CNBC-TV18 via Moneycontrol, “There are no delays as far as we are concerned. We remain completely on track.” Moneycontrol

MTAR shares bounced after dropping 15.55% in the last two sessions, Business Standard said. Traders pulled back on the stock, worried by no new project updates and a possible slip in demand for MTAR parts. The same report said MTAR’s Wyoming project is still slated to start in early 2028, with or without Crusoe as partner. That eased worries about near-term risk to the clean-energy pipeline. Business Standard

MTAR shares are getting support from the numbers. Sales for the March quarter jumped 67.2% to ₹306 crore and net profit climbed 222.6% to ₹44.2 crore. The maker of precision parts for clean energy and defence lifted its FY27 revenue growth forecast to 80% from 50%, Moneycontrol reported. At the end of FY26, MTAR had an order book of ₹2,581.9 crore and is targeting ₹5,000 crore by FY27. Motilal Oswal analysts told Moneycontrol they see a bigger Oracle-Bloom deal possibly bringing in another ₹1,400–1,700 crore in orders for MTAR. Moneycontrol

Questions on valuation and dependence haven’t gone away. MTAR trades at a trailing P/E near 240.6 and a price-to-book ratio of 27.5, according to Sharekhan. The stock is far off its 52-week low at ₹1,390.50 and not far from its ₹8,449.50 high. That big P/E says investors see sharp growth ahead. If MTAR misses on Bloom, execution, margin or working capital, it could get hit. Sharekhan

MTAR is trading more on risk than fundamentals right now. Growth stories are active, with new clean energy orders, more nuclear projects, and a higher FY27 outlook. Still, the stock has run up and trades at over 240x earnings. Management needs to deliver on its targets to support this level. The focus is on order flow from Bloom, clean-energy contracts turning into revenue, then the coming FY27 update. If MTAR reports strong numbers, the high multiple could hold. If not, there’s a risk of a sharp correction.

data.title: Tata Motors Focuses on EVs and SUVs, JLR Still a Concern for FY26
data.description:
Mumbai, June 16, 2026, 18:45 (IST)

  • Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles broke its domestic sales record in FY26 as SUV, EV and CNG demand stayed strong.
  • Jaguar Land Rover weighed on consolidated results as a cyber issue, tariffs, and softer demand in China hit the unit.
  • The company wants to grab 18–20% share of the domestic market and is chasing a double-digit EBITDA margin. Plans call for five new EV models in the lineup by FY30.

Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles is aiming for faster growth in FY27, coming off record India sales in FY26 and with Jaguar Land Rover problems still in play. The automaker pushed out 6.42 lakh cars and SUVs in FY26, up 15.3% from a year earlier, beating the market by a wide margin, but JLR kept having trouble. “We enter FY27 with confidence, supported by a robust pipeline of new launches and multi-powertrain offerings,” Chairman N Chandrasekaran said, according to Reuters.

Tata Motors’ domestic business led growth, with the PV revenue up 20.7% at Rs 58,465 crore for FY25, Moneycontrol reported. Profit before tax and exceptions rose 32.6% to Rs 1,436 crore, pushing market share to 13.6%. Tata Motors finished the year holding net cash of Rs 6,710 crore. MD and CEO Shailesh Chandra said a “consciously sharpened strategy” on launches and powertrain optimisation was behind the gains. Moneycontrol

Tata is keeping its multi-fuel strategy, launching SUVs, CNG cars and EVs rather than committing to a single path. The company’s EV sales rose 43.4% in FY26 to more than 92,000 units. Total EV sales have now topped 2.5 lakh. Tata holds 40.2% of India’s EV market and has led the segment for over seven years, The Economic Times reported. Reuters reported Tata Motors PV will invest Rs 330-350 billion from FY26 to FY30 in its passenger vehicles and EVs, aiming for an 18–20% domestic market share and a double-digit EBITDA margin. EBITDA stands for earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation. The Economic Times

Tata is still having trouble with JLR. The British luxury arm’s FY26 revenue dropped 20.9% to £22.91 billion after it missed five weeks of output because of a cyberattack and took hits from new US tariffs, soft China demand, higher marketing spend, and pricier commodities. JLR has also begun winding down older Jaguar cars. Tata Motors PV reported consolidated FY26 revenue of Rs 3,35,582 crore. Loss before tax from continuing operations, after exceptions, was Rs 1,600 crore. Consolidated net debt rose to Rs 30,700 crore, with most of the rise tied to weak free cash flow after JLR’s lost production. The Economic Times

Tata goes into next year in India with momentum from the Sierra launch, Nexon and Punch sales, plus gains in EV market share and its retail base. But over at JLR, the company still needs to prove it can offset tariffs and weak China demand with steady production, more cost cutting, and new models like the New Range Rover Electric and Jaguar Type 01. Tata said JLR aims to return breakeven volumes to around 3 lakh units in two years. The Economic Times

Tata Motors is doubling down on its brand play. Chairman Chandrasekaran said the company’s passenger vehicle arm and JLR will share more on manufacturing, tech and staff. He wants to build “distinctive, trusted and aspirational brands” that people want to buy. Tata is putting more attention on its passenger vehicle brand, but profit, EV spend and JLR’s pace of recovery are still priorities. The Economic Times

data.title: Stocks climb as oil retreats on U.S.-Iran Hormuz deal; Friday agreement expected
data.description:
New York, June 15, 2026, 17:09 (EDT)

  • Brent crude settled 4.76% lower at $83.17 a barrel. The drop came after the U.S. and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
  • U.S. stocks rallied. The S&P 500 climbed 1.7%. The Dow ended at a new all-time high.
  • Investors are waiting to see if Friday’s planned signing in Geneva brings any normalization to shipping and supply.

Indexes rose Monday as investors bet on a possible U.S.-Iran deal that could defuse tensions and get the Strait of Hormuz moving again. Stocks bounced after recent losses tied to inflation worries. Brent crude dropped $4.16, or 4.76%, to settle at $83.17. U.S. West Texas Intermediate fell $4.13, or 4.87%, ending the day at $80.75. Both crude benchmarks closed at their lowest since March 4, Reuters said in its oil market report. Reuters

Stocks climbed, tracking a drop in oil prices that offered some relief to companies and consumers facing higher fuel, freight, and production costs. The Dow Jones Industrial Average added 468.77 points to 51,671.03. The S&P 500 was up 122.83 at 7,554.29. The Nasdaq Composite moved higher by 795.10 points to 26,683.94, AP reported. Airlines and cruise lines were out front, both sectors sensitive to fuel swings. AP News

Peace deal remains out of reach as a draft heads for a Friday signing in Geneva, Reuters reported. Iran’s Mehr news agency said Hormuz could reopen inside 30 days, but only if Iranian terms are met. Traders are watching for any signals on when ships could really move again, if insurance will cover cargoes, and if a shaky truce will hold with nuclear talks in sight. “The peace framework is the clearest sign yet that both Washington and Iran want an off-ramp…but this is still a framework, not a final deal,” Charu Chanana, chief investment strategist at Saxo in Singapore, told Reuters, according to Investing. Investing

J.P. Morgan Asset Management’s Karen Ward told OilPrice.com crude’s drop could be a “massive tailwind” for global equities. Ward said cheaper oil could broaden the rally past tech stocks and help central banks hold off on more rate hikes. On Monday, the bond market moved that way. Reuters said U.S. Treasury yields fell as traders took falling oil as a signal inflation may be easing. Oilprice

Bearish tone sticks. Reuters reported more than 14 million barrels a day of oil is still offline. Getting production and refining back to pre-war levels could take weeks or even months. Neil Crosby at Sparta Commodities said the Gulf’s vessel supply chain and restarts won’t be easy to manage. He noted some shipowners may wait for insurers to clear safety before moving. Reuters

Stocks rallied on the Hormuz deal, with traders betting it’s positive for growth and risk assets. But plenty say the situation is unsettled. Attention shifts to the Geneva signing planned for Friday. A bigger test comes when actual oil and gas flows through Hormuz. Until then, the market move is pinned on expectations for cheaper energy to ease inflation, if supply remains steady.

data.title: Billionbrains Garage Ventures Gains 4% as Groww Moves Toward FTSE Index Entry
data.description:
Mumbai, June 16, 2026, 03:38 IST

  • Billionbrains Garage Ventures, parent to Groww and trading as GROWW on the NSE and 544603 on the BSE, finished at ₹207.34 on June 15, rising 4.21%. Total volume across both exchanges came in at 84.9 million shares. Trendlyne
  • Indian shares had a strong session. The Nifty 50 rose 0.98% and the Sensex gained 0.97% as oil prices dropped and global risk appetite picked up. Reuters
  • Groww is set to join the FTSE Global Equity Index Series on June 22, one of the Indian names added in the latest round of FTSE global index changes.

Billionbrains Garage Ventures shares jumped Monday, finishing the session at ₹207.34, up ₹8.37. The stock traded close to the top of the day’s ₹198.81–₹208.00 range. It’s now near its 52-week high of ₹227.20. Market cap at the close was about ₹1.30 lakh crore. Turnover was high, so the price move came with strong volume instead of light trading. Groww

The stock got a boost from the broad market rally. Reuters said Indian shares climbed as the U.S. and Iran reached a preliminary deal to stop the war and allow ships through the Strait of Hormuz, which brought Brent crude down. Cheaper oil is usually good for India since it buys most of its crude, taking some strain off inflation, the rupee, and the trade gap. “Now that the Iran war appears to be nearing an end, investors have a significant source of comfort,” Monarch Networth Capital CEO Gaurav Bhandari told Reuters. Reuters

NDTV Profit pointed to a technical call from Deven Mehata, Manager – Technical & Derivatives Analyst at IDBI Capital Market and Securities, who told investors to buy Groww at ₹198.97 with a ₹210 target and a ₹193 stop loss. A stop loss is meant to cap losses for traders. With Groww ending at ₹207.34, shares are now near that target, so further upside could need more buying and not just the move seen on Monday. NDTV Profit

Groww bulls point to scale and numbers from the latest quarter. The company posted a consolidated net profit of ₹686 crore for the March quarter, which is up 122% year-on-year. Revenue from operations climbed 87% to ₹1,505 crore. According to The Economic Times, growth got a lift from user activity, more derivatives trading, higher SIP flows and expansion into new lines like margin trading facility, or MTF, where clients use securities as collateral for bigger bets. The Economic Times

Valuation and cyclicality weigh on the bear case here. ICICI Direct shows the stock trading at a trailing price-to-earnings ratio of 62.47 and a price-to-book ratio of 13.47. P/E compares the stock price with earnings per share, and P/B looks at market price versus book value. Those are high for a company exposed to trading cycles and shifting market sentiment. Economic Times flagged the risk from a weaker market, which could slow user growth and inflows. Based on these numbers, the stock looks fairly valued to risky—not outright cheap—unless earnings or market-share growth jumps again. ICICI Direct

Index changes are in focus next. FTSE Russell said its June quarterly review goes live Monday, June 22, after markets close on Friday, June 19. According to Upstox, Billionbrains Garage Ventures, known as Groww, is set to be added to FTSE global indices. Passive funds tracking the indices often adjust their holdings around these changes. That can push up demand ahead of the effective date, but profit-taking after the event is a risk as traders “sell the news.”

data.title: Sensex, Nifty End Up As FIIs Return After US-Iran Agreement
data.description:
Mumbai, June 15, 2026, 19:19 IST.

  • Sensex climbed 736.38 points to settle at 76,264.33. Nifty ended 231 points higher at 23,853.90.
  • FIIs ended their run of selling and turned net buyers in Indian equities, with net purchases of ₹200.05 crore after 11 straight sessions of outflows.
  • Brent crude fell about 5%, trading around $83 a barrel as the US-Iran agreement eased concerns about Gulf oil supply.

Softer crude and less concern over West Asia drove Indian equities higher Monday, sending the Sensex up 736.38 points, or 0.97%, to 76,264.33 at the close. The Nifty put on 231 points, or 0.98%, to close at 23,853.90, according to market data. The up move was supported by buying, with both Moneycontrol and Telegraph India reporting the gains.

Foreign investors swung to buying, taking in ₹15,650.20 crore and selling ₹15,450.15 crore, for a net inflow of ₹200.05 crore, Moneycontrol said, citing provisional exchange data. Domestic institutions were bigger buyers, with net purchases of ₹3,189.26 crore. Steady institutional buying can build up rallies in a way short-covering usually doesn’t. Moneycontrol

Brent crude hit a three-month low after news from Reuters that the U.S. and Iran have a preliminary agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and call a halt to three months of fighting. Risk appetite in Europe climbed, with Reuters reporting a new record for the STOXX 600. Reuters

Cheaper crude is a boost for India, trimming the import bill, easing inflation, and lending support to the rupee. Moneycontrol said oil marketing firms and fuel-intensive sectors did well with lower oil prices, but shares of upstream energy names underperformed. Reliance Industries, Mahindra & Mahindra, and Maruti Suzuki pushed the benchmark index higher. Moneycontrol

Sector gains were broad, but some stocks lagged. PTI via Telegraph India reported Trent, InterGlobe Aviation, Bajaj Finserv, UltraTech Cement, Eternal, and Maruti were top movers. NTPC, ICICI Bank, Asian Paints, and Hindustan Unilever fell behind. Rajesh Palviya at Axis Direct called easing tensions a “significant positive for global risk assets,” and pointed to lower crude prices as a lift for big importers. Telegraph India

All eyes are on the peace deal, with markets waiting to see if it gets signed June 19 and if oil moves normally again through the Strait of Hormuz. The Guardian reported that big issues remain, including when the strait will open and what rules will let ships pass. India’s stocks could hold gains if crude stays down and foreign buyers keep coming. Any trouble with the deal could shake oil, the rupee and foreign flows quickly. Theguardian

data.title: IFCI reaches new 52-week peak after NSE IPO talk
data.description:
Mumbai, June 15, 2026, 17:35 IST

  • IFCI jumped 5.95% to end at ₹89.60 on the NSE after hitting ₹91.30 during the day. On the BSE, shares finished at ₹89.52, up 5.78%.
  • IFCI shares moved up as traders cited talk that NSE could file its IPO draft this week. Many see IFCI as a listed route to invest in an NSE listing.
  • The latest rally has made the stock more of a momentum play and a riskier bet, rather than just a cheap pick.

IFCI Limited shares finished up on Monday, gaining 5.95% to settle at ₹89.60 on the NSE. The stock hit a high of ₹91.30 during the session as traders jumped in ahead of the National Stock Exchange IPO. On the BSE, IFCI closed a bit lower at ₹89.52, up 5.78%, after reaching ₹91.36 intraday. Trading was busy on the NSE, with turnover hitting about 309.8 million shares, Mirae Asset Sharekhan showed. Sharekhan

IFCI shares barely reacted to the latest earnings, but volumes jumped after word got around that NSE might file its draft red herring prospectus (DRHP) with SEBI this week. The DRHP is a first step to listing. IFCI is drawing attention because it holds a 52.86% stake in Stock Holding Corporation of India Ltd, which owns about 4.4% of NSE. This indirect exposure is making IFCI a trade for those speculating on the NSE IPO. IFCI Business Standard

Stocks climb when buyers spend up for earnings, assets, or hope for payouts. They slide if optimism cools, values look stretched, or risk pops up. For IFCI, it’s all about the NSE IPO; with the process moving, traders may value IFCI higher because it owns a piece of NSE. The next thing to look for is the DRHP, offer-for-sale details, or anything from the NSE IPO that hints at pricing. Reports today said the NSE IPO will be an offer for sale—so current holders want to sell, and NSE won’t raise new money. Moneycontrol Business Standard

Bears have more to point to after IFCI’s Q4 FY26 results. Operating revenue on a consolidated basis was up at ₹470.43 crore, but net profit came in sharply lower at ₹34.06 crore, down from ₹260.43 crore last year. ICICI Direct noted gross NPAs of ₹3,589.97 crore and a gross NPA ratio of 95.79%. Asset quality is weak. CRAR landed at negative 18.78%, below regulatory norms. ICICI Direct

IFCI has looked stretched after a sharp move, trading at close to 126 times trailing earnings and 2.55 times book, with zero dividend yield, according to Sharekhan. Virat Jagad, Senior Technical Research Analyst at Bonanza, told Mint the stock hit “the crucial resistance zone around 90.” The RSI is sitting above 70, showing momentum is strong, though Jagad flagged risk for a short-term pullback here. RSI can track when trades run hot. Sharekhan mint

IFCI is mainly for investors eyeing gains from the NSE IPO. The stock made a sharp run, so it now looks less interesting for those being cautious. Much of the good news seems baked in. Any issues with the NSE filing, weak IPO appetite, or problems with IFCI’s exit could see some holders take profits. There’s a reason to stick with it, but at current levels it’s more a bet than a bargain.

data.title: Deccan Gold Mines Hits Record After SC Clears Ganajur Mine
data.description:
Mumbai, June 15, 2026, 18:38 IST

  • Deccan Gold Mines hit a fresh record high as the Supreme Court put out an order strengthening the legal standing of mining lease applicants who filed before 2015.
  • The stock closed higher, but statutory clearances are still required before mining operations can begin under the ruling.

Deccan Gold Mines surged Monday after the Supreme Court sided with mining lease applicants whose decisions were taken before India’s 2015 mining law update. The move pushed Deccan Gold to record highs. Shares closed at ₹193.05 on the BSE, up 17.79%. The stock touched ₹196.65 earlier in the session. On the NSE, shares were last at ₹192.61, up 17.62%. Market value was close to ₹3,820 crore, according to Business Standard’s market data. Business Standard

Supreme Court move lifts stocks; Deccan Gold Mines looks at Ganajur order Markets climbed after the Supreme Court said on June 9 that pre-2015 mining lease rights aren’t just pending applications under the changed 2015 Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Act. ScanX reported Deccan Gold Mines is now reviewing what the ruling could mean for its Ganajur project in Karnataka. Scanx

Ganajur is again in the spotlight as one of Deccan Gold’s stuck projects. The Economic Times reported the company lists about 3.08 lakh ounces of gold resources there. The same report said Deccan Exploration Services, a Deccan Gold subsidiary, had applied for permits before mining rights were switched to the auction route. The Economic Times

Investors kept buying, with recent technical signals boosting the action. MarketsMojo noted during the session the stock held above important moving averages and kept beating the Sensex. That performance tends to bring in short-term buyers, especially after a legal update settled questions around a major project. MarketsMojo

Ganajur isn’t about to start producing gold just because of the rise. Statutory approvals are still missing after the Supreme Court decision, ScanX said, so actual work can’t kick off yet. Those approvals cover mining plans, environmental clearances, or forest permits where needed. So even with the legal win, there’s still execution risk hanging over the project. Scanx

Investors are waiting for Deccan Gold Mines’ next update on Ganajur, which is likely to act as the stock’s main driver. Focus will be on what the company says about the effects of the court ruling and regulatory clearances. For now, the recent rally in the shares shows investors are rethinking legal risk, rather than reacting to any firm change in production timing or cash flows.

data.title: Satellite Images of China’s ‘Military City’ Fuel Nuclear Fears
data.description:
Beijing, June 9, 2026, 01:46 CST

  • Satellite imagery highlights a major fortified military facility southwest of Beijing, along with a broader missile support network out in China’s northwest.
  • With China ramping up pressure on Taiwan and expanding nuclear sites that are plainly visible in commercial satellite images, the issue has taken on a new urgency.
  • Analysts warn that the real danger lies in misjudging motives. Even when measures are meant to bolster defenses, they can easily be mistaken for gearing up for a more severe crisis.

Fresh satellite images have thrown a spotlight on China’s expanding military footprint—first, with what appears to be a sprawling underground command facility outside Beijing; and in another development, the discovery of launch pads and support structures clustered around nuclear missile silos out in the northwest.

This isn’t just about a single location. It’s the broader trend. With reinforced command bunkers, missile-support installations, and stepped-up Chinese military activity near Taiwan, U.S. and allied strategists face a more complicated question: Is Beijing primarily focused on making its forces tougher to target, or is it gearing up for something bigger with Washington?

The Financial Times said China is working on a site about 1,500 acres in size, situated roughly 30 km southwest of Beijing—current and former U.S. officials see it as a possible wartime command center, and potentially much bigger than the Pentagon. Futura-Sciences, citing its May 20 report, pointed to satellite images that have reignited focus on what it calls a vast underground military command facility near Beijing.

Satellite images reviewed by Reuters as of May 29 reveal over 80 launch pads and three octagonal structures in the vicinity of the Hami nuclear silo field, Xinjiang. “Grand scale,” was how Alexander Neill, adjunct fellow at Pacific Forum, described the ongoing construction. Hans Kristensen from the Federation of American Scientists pointed to an “extraordinary effort.” Tong Zhao at the Carnegie Endowment noted there’s a “real possibility” that some of these facilities relate to command, control and communications, or C3—the vital network linking top commanders and military units. Reuters

This is key: China’s nuclear strategy depends on maintaining a reliable second-strike capability—the assurance it can retaliate after absorbing a first blow. Extra fortified facilities, new mobile-launch infrastructure, and stronger communications nodes would complicate attempts to neutralize that arsenal in a crisis.

Beijing maintains its nuclear stance is purely defensive, pointing to its no-first-use pledge—meaning it claims it won’t initiate a nuclear conflict. According to a draft Pentagon report reviewed by Reuters in December, China has probably armed over 100 intercontinental ballistic missiles (ICBMs) in newly constructed silo fields and appears on pace to surpass 1,000 nuclear warheads by 2030. ICBMs are long-range missiles capable of striking targets thousands of miles from launch.

Still, there’s a major caveat here. Five security scholars told Reuters the desert infrastructure might be tied to China’s nuclear program, but a lot of crucial information is missing—there’s no clarity on which weapons would be launched from these pads, or if the octagon-shaped sites are sheltering truck-mounted missiles or just auxiliary equipment. That lack of certainty is itself a risk: it opens the door for outsiders to exaggerate what’s happening, or for governments to dismiss what could actually be a significant development.

The rivalry here is between the United States and Russia—both countries continue to hold far bigger nuclear stockpiles. Still, what’s catching the attention of analysts and U.S. officials is the pace and secrecy behind China’s buildup, especially now that the final U.S.-Russia nuclear arms-control agreements are coming under pressure.

Taiwan isn’t a distant concern. As Reuters noted Monday, Chinese military pressure ratcheted up while Nvidia, Intel, and SK Group leaders gathered in Taipei for Computex. The island’s defence ministry counted 79 Chinese warplanes in the vicinity over June 2-5, right as the tech event ran. “Markets and governments are underestimating the risk,” said Hudson Institute senior fellow David Feith. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang weighed in too, saying supply chains have to be “diversified and redundant.” Reuters

The South China Sea is back in focus. Reuters flagged satellite images last week that appeared to show some kind of suspected structure at the entrance to Scarborough Shoal, a contested atoll both China and the Philippines claim. Later shots indicated whatever it was had disappeared. China’s foreign ministry maintained that Beijing holds “indisputable sovereignty” over the site, but stopped short of saying whether China had installed the object. Reuters

China’s diplomatic efforts are running alongside other developments. On Monday, President Xi Jinping assured North Korean leader Kim Jong Un that Beijing’s backing for Pyongyang remains firm. Craig Singleton of the Foundation for Defense of Democracies pointed out the summit underlined China’s view of North Korea as a “strategic asset.” Reuters

At this point, satellites reveal what China can do, not what it plans to do. The images don’t demonstrate that Beijing is gearing up for conflict. What they do confirm is significant Chinese spending on hardened command centers and nuclear facilities—assets that would come into play if tensions with the United States, Taiwan, or nearby partners escalated.
